notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
the sim opens on Spectral Schematics, which the field rarely builds (0.2% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Renna (it never builds Entropy, Overseer) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for 10s rotation vs squishies: first spike lands around minute 12 (Spectral Schematics), third item by minute 24. Take Demiurge: +19.5% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

augments.note: converts Mortal Coil execute into an ally self-heal zone; team-side rework

metaBuilds.title: Magical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 24 — about 892 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +23% more one-combo burst: try Spectral Schematics over Entropy (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Skirmisher
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for 10s rotation damage, online around minute 24 — about 947 damage over a 10s rotation in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +74% more 10s rotation damage: try Spectral Schematics over Entropy (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Magical Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 5,527 effective HP vs physical in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 6% of our best core on physical survivability. The winrate is earned, not luck.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. The Fey deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Wraith Leggings: their burst combo hits 20% less hard, and this kit gives up 25% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Whenever this lane is not even, the winnable fight is yours: play aggressive, but respect jungle attention. Argus deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Wraith Leggings: their burst combo hits 23% less hard, and this kit gives up 25% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. Iggy & Scorch deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Wraith Leggings: their burst combo hits 20% less hard, and this kit gives up 25% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Gideon deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Wraith Leggings: their burst combo hits 23% less hard, and this kit gives up 25% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Countess deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Wraith Leggings: their burst combo hits 18% less hard, and this kit gives up 24% of its damage for it.

matchups.gameplan: Your window is early: force trades before their core completes, and do not coin-flip fights past the crossover. Lt. Belica executes low targets with Neural Disruptor — don't linger below half HP.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Wraith Leggings: their burst combo hits 18% less hard, and this kit gives up 25% of its damage for it.

laneSteer.provenance: “Netherward” ⇒ sustain; the sim models this augment — field midlane 53.1% over 6,243 games.
